name: Container Offices
description:
Container Office — ISO-Grade, Stackable, Pan India Delivery

Two SAMAN container offices installed at a large infrastructure project site. IS 2062 Corten steel fabrication. Delivered and erected Pan India in 7–21 working days.

If you are procuring container offices for a large construction project, corporate branch setup, industrial park, port facility, or government infrastructure site — this page covers what you need to evaluate SAMAN as a supplier.

We manufacture and supply ISO-grade container offices from our facilities in Bangalore and Greater Noida. 20-ft and 40-ft units. Single level or stacked. Delivered and erected Pan India in 7–21 working days depending on project scale and location.

Who This Is For — Project Types We Supply

Container offices at this scale are ordered by procurement teams running significant projects, not individual buyers.

EPC and infrastructure contractors running highway, dam, power, or metro projects — where a team of 20 to 200 engineers needs a functional site office complex for 12 to 36 months.

Corporate facilities teams setting up branch offices at locations where permanent construction is not feasible or not justified within the project budget cycle.

Port operators and industrial parks that need permanent-feeling but relocatable office infrastructure for operations, administration, and on-site management.

Government and institutional projects where procurement requires IS certification, material test documentation, and GST-compliant invoicing before an order is approved.

If you need a smaller-scale cabin-based workspace for a 4–6 person team, that is a different product. See our portable office range for that requirement.

Container Office Specifications — What You Are Procuring

Every SAMAN container office is fabricated from new ISO-grade Corten steel. These are not repurposed shipping containers — they are purpose-built office units fabricated to container form factor standards.

Full procurement specification:

  • ISO corner castings: Standard 4-corner ISO fittings — 2-level stacking rated, crane-liftable via standard 4-point lifting chains
  • Frame: IS 2062 Corten steel, 3mm minimum wall thickness
  • Stacking capacity: Rated to 2-level with structural interconnection — requires 150mm M20 reinforced concrete pad foundation
  • Crane lifting: 40-ton telescopic crane minimum for loaded 40-ft units
  • Internal dimensions (20-ft unit): 5,500mm × 2,200mm × 2,400mm usable height
  • Internal dimensions (40-ft unit): 11,800mm × 2,200mm × 2,400mm usable height
  • Insulation: 50mm PUF insulated panels — maintains 8–10°C differential from outside temperature
  • Electrical: 240V single-phase wiring, switchboard, MCB panel, lighting fixtures as standard

Standard ISO corner fittings on every SAMAN container office — 2-level stacking rated and 4-point crane-liftable. IS 2062 Corten steel construction.

Custom specifications — additional windows, AC provision, toilet attachment, network conduit, generator socket — are fabricated at our factory before dispatch.

Single Unit or Multi-Unit — How to Configure Your Project

Most large projects require multiple units. The configuration decision determines your foundation requirement, delivery sequence, and total project cost.

Single 20-ft unit: 4–6 person team. Compacted gravel or concrete pad at four support points. Crane-placed in under 2 hours.

Single 40-ft unit: 8–12 person team. Same foundation requirement. Requires a 40-ton telescopic crane for placement.

Side-by-side multi-unit (ground level): Two or more units connected with common wall cut-through. Creates larger floor area without stacking. Foundation requirement same as individual units. Our modular container office page covers multi-unit ground-level configurations in detail.

Stacked (2-level): Doubles floor area on the same footprint. Requires reinforced concrete pad foundation — minimum 150mm M20 grade across the full stacked footprint. Structural interconnection between levels is mandatory — we handle this during erection. External staircase included. See our modular shipping container office page for stacked configuration details.

Two-level stacked container office installation. 150mm M20 concrete pad foundation required. External staircase included. Doubles floor area on the same site footprint.

New vs. Refurbished — Which Container Grade Is Right

New ISO-fabricated containers — what we supply as standard: All SAMAN container offices are new fabrication. No prior use as cargo or storage. Material test certificates, IS 2062 grade confirmation, and warranty documentation available for every unit. Required for government procurement, port operations, and projects with third-party inspection requirements.

Refurbished containers — available on request: Structurally sound used shipping containers converted to office use. Typically 25–35% lower cost than new fabrication. Acceptable for short-duration projects where procurement documentation is not mandatory. Lead time is 10–25 working days depending on availability.

For most corporate and government procurement, new fabrication is the right choice. For 6-month highway projects where cost-per-day is the primary driver, refurbished is worth evaluating. Tell us your project type and we will advise which grade makes financial sense.

Delivery Across India — How Multi-Unit Projects Are Coordinated

Container offices are heavy, high-value units. Multi-unit delivery for large projects is coordinated differently from single-unit orders.

SAMAN erection team placing a container office unit at site. Our team handles delivery, crane coordination, levelling, electrical commissioning, and handover. You arrange crane access — we handle everything else.

Before dispatch, we confirm road access to your site, crane availability on your end, and foundation readiness. Units are fabricated and dispatched in coordinated batches — not one at a time — so your site receives multiple units aligned with your erection schedule rather than in random sequence across several weeks.

Our erection team arrives with the delivery. They place, level, interconnect, and commission all electrical. You arrange crane access; we handle everything from that point forward. Completion is marked by a site walk-through, checklist sign-off, and documentation handover.

Transit time from our Bangalore or Greater Noida facility runs 3–10 working days depending on destination state. Erection adds 1–3 days per batch. Total project commissioning timeline is confirmed at the order stage — not estimated after the fact.

For urgent single-unit requirements with a short lead time, our mobile container office is our fastest-dispatch configuration.

Rent or Buy — The Financial Case for Container Projects

Container offices are available for both monthly rental and outright purchase. The right choice depends on project duration, number of concurrent sites, and whether the asset can be capitalized in your budget.

The purchase cost of a 20-ft container office recovers in approximately 8–10 months of equivalent monthly rental. Projects running longer than that duration are buying at a net cost lower than renting — before accounting for resale value.

Resale recovery changes the calculation significantly. Container offices retain 40–60% of purchase value after 3 years of use. A procurement team running a 3-year budget cycle that factors in resale is effectively paying 40–60% of sticker price for 3 years of ownership. No other temporary office solution offers that resale floor.

Rental makes financial sense when the project runs under 8 months, involves a single location, or requires flexibility to exit without asset disposal. Purchase makes financial sense when the project runs 9 months or longer, the same units will move across multiple project sites, or the asset can be depreciated on the company balance sheet.

When you have confirmed the configuration and are ready to place an order, the buy container office page covers current stock, pricing by unit, and payment terms.

Price and What Affects Your Quote

A standard 20-ft container office starts at Rs 2,85,000 — new ISO-grade fabrication, electrical fitted, delivered within 200 km of Bangalore or Greater Noida.

A 40-ft unit starts at Rs 4,20,000 under the same conditions.

What increases the quote: distance beyond 200 km, stacking requirement (adds foundation specification and structural interconnection work), AC fitout, toilet attachment, generator provision, premium interior finish, or multi-unit simultaneous delivery with dedicated erection team.

What does not change: IS 2062 grade steel, factory testing before dispatch, and erection at site are included in every quote. No separate erection invoice.

What SAMAN Provides With Every Order

For procurement teams that need documentation before releasing a purchase order:

  • IS 2062 material test certificate for structural steel
  • Factory inspection record — pre-dispatch checklist
  • GST-compliant tax invoice
  • 12-month structural warranty on new fabrication units
  • After-erection inspection report

Interior of a SAMAN 20-ft container office — LED lighting, split AC, built-in workstations, and white panel walls. Factory-fitted before dispatch. Material test certificates and IS certification included with every order.

Documentation is provided as standard — you do not need to request it separately. If your project requires third-party inspection at the factory before dispatch, we accommodate that with advance notice.

Frequently Asked Questions

What certifications confirm a SAMAN container office meets procurement requirements?

Every unit is fabricated from IS 2062 Corten steel with material test certificates provided as standard. Units are built to ISO container form factor with rated corner castings for 2-level stacking and 4-point crane lifting. Structural warranty is 12 months on new fabrication. For buyers comparing container offices against cabin-based alternatives, the procurement difference is structural grade, stacking capability, and documentation — not interior fitout.

Can SAMAN supply stacked container offices — what foundation is required?

Yes. Two-level stacking is standard. You need a reinforced concrete pad foundation — minimum 150mm depth, M20 grade — across the full footprint of the stacked units. We provide foundation drawings with the order. A 40-ton telescopic crane is required on site for placement. We erect and structurally interconnect the levels. You arrange the crane.

What is the minimum order for Pan India delivery projects?

No minimum for single-unit orders within South India or Delhi NCR. For Pan India projects outside our primary delivery zones, we recommend a minimum of 3 units per delivery batch to keep logistics costs proportionate to unit value.

How long does a container office last and what maintenance does it need?

A new-fabrication IS 2062 Corten steel container office has a working life of 15–20 years with standard maintenance. Corten steel develops a protective oxidation layer that resists further corrosion — no annual repainting required. Panels and electrical components can be replaced independently without structural intervention.

Can container offices be relocated after the project ends?

Yes. ISO corner fittings are standard on all units — any crane operator with standard lifting chains can lift and load. Most units are relocated 2–4 times over their working life without structural degradation. We can help coordinate transport when your project closes.

Is refurbished stock available if our project budget does not allow new fabrication?

Yes, on request. Refurbished units — structurally sound used shipping containers converted to office spec — are available at approximately 25–35% below new fabrication cost. Lead time is 10–25 working days depending on availability. Material test certification is not available on refurbished units.

Ready to get a project quote?

Share your site location, number of units, configuration requirement, and delivery date. We will respond with a complete itemized quote within 24 hours — covering units, transport, erection, and documentation.

Call or WhatsApp: +91 97089 89937
